name: CLI Entry Point
description: Click-based CLI surface for all arbiter commands. Thin adapter layer that validates inputs via custom ParamTypes, constructs Pydantic request models, delegates to engine functions, formats Pydantic response models, and outputs results with appropriate exit codes. Top-level `arbiter` Click group with sub-groups `trust` and `canary`, plus top-level commands `init`, `register`, `authority`, `blast-radius`, `soak`, `report`, `watch`, `findings`, `conflicts`. Shared CliContext on ctx.obj carries registry_path, config, and output_format. Custom ArbiterGroup.invoke() wraps super().invoke() in try/except for centralized error handling with node/field context in messages. All errors to stderr. JSON output is .model_dump(mode='json') of response models. Text output is human-readable formatted string. --format supports text|json with TTY auto-detection.

types.name: ExitCode
types.description: CLI exit codes. Every command contract specifies which subset it can produce.

types.name: OutputFormat
types.description: Output format for CLI responses. 'auto' detects TTY (text if TTY, json otherwise).

types.name: ArbiterConfig

fields.name: ledger_checksum_interval
fields.description: Number of ledger entries between SHA256 checksum lines.

fields.name: http_port
fields.description: Default HTTP API port for watch mode.

fields.name: otlp_port
fields.description: Default OTLP gRPC port for watch mode.

fields.name: cold_start_timeout_s
fields.description: Maximum seconds for watch cold start.

types.description: Top-level arbiter configuration loaded from YAML. Delegated from config component.

types.name: CliContext

fields.name: registry_path
fields.description: Resolved absolute path to the registry directory.

types.description: Shared CLI context attached to click ctx.obj. Loaded in the top-level group callback. Available to all subcommands via @click.pass_obj.

types.name: NodeId
types.description: Opaque string identifying a node in the access graph. Validated by NodeIdType ParamType: non-empty, matches ^[a-zA-Z0-9_.-]+$ pattern.

types.name: RunId
types.description: Opaque string identifying a run (canary run, report run). Validated by RunIdType ParamType: non-empty UUID v4 format.

types.name: TierName
types.description: String name of a data classification tier. Validated by TierType ParamType against the classification registry enum.

types.name: VersionTag
types.description: Semver-compatible version string. Validated by VersionType ParamType: matches ^\d+\.\d+\.\d+(-[a-zA-Z0-9.]+)?$ pattern.

types.name: ReviewId
types.description: Opaque string identifying a review for taint reset. Non-empty string.

fields.name: registry_path
fields.description: Path where the registry directory should be created.

fields.name: force
fields.description: If true, re-initialize even if registry exists.

fields.name: registry_path
fields.description: Absolute path to the initialized registry directory.

fields.name: created_files

types.description: List of file paths created during initialization.

fields.name: nodes_ingested
fields.description: Number of nodes ingested from the access graph.

fields.name: edges_ingested
fields.description: Number of edges ingested from the access graph.

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The node to query trust for.

types.description: Ordered list of trust score entries, oldest first.

fields.name: tainted

fields.name: node_id

fields.name: node_id
fields.description: The node whose taint was cleared.

fields.name: run_id

functions.name: arbiter_group
functions.description: Top-level Click group callback. Loads config from --config path (default: ./arbiter.yaml), resolves --registry-path, resolves --format (auto → text if TTY, json otherwise), constructs CliContext and attaches to ctx.obj. Uses custom ArbiterGroup class whose invoke() wraps super().invoke() in try/except to catch all domain exceptions, format CliError, write to stderr, and sys.exit with mapped ExitCode.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Path to arbiter YAML config file.

inputs.name: registry_path
inputs.description: Override registry path from config.

inputs.name: format
inputs.description: Output format: text, json, or auto (TTY detection).

error_cases.name: config_not_found
error_cases.condition: Config file path does not exist and is not the default

error_cases.name: config_invalid_yaml
error_cases.condition: Config file is not valid YAML or fails ArbiterConfig validation

error_cases.name: registry_path_invalid
error_cases.condition: Explicit registry_path does not exist or is not a directory

preconditions: config_path must point to a readable YAML file or not exist (defaults used)
If registry_path is provided, it must be a valid directory path
postconditions: CliContext is fully initialized with resolved output_format (never 'auto')
CliContext is attached to click ctx.obj
Config is loaded and validated as ArbiterConfig

structured_side_effects.description: Reads and parses arbiter configuration from YAML.

functions.name: cmd_init
functions.description: Command: `arbiter init [--force]`. Initializes registry directory, empty trust ledger (JSONL with checksum), and default config (FA-A-001). Delegates to registry.initialize_registry.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context from group callback.

inputs.name: force
inputs.description: Re-initialize even if registry already exists.

error_cases.name: already_initialized
error_cases.condition: Registry directory exists and force is false

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: Cannot create registry directory due to filesystem permissions

error_cases.name: parent_not_found
error_cases.condition: Parent directory of registry_path does not exist

preconditions: CliContext is initialized
Parent directory of registry_path must be writable
postconditions: Registry directory exists at registry_path
Empty trust ledger file exists within registry
Default config file exists within registry
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.target: registry directory and initial files (ledger, config)
structured_side_effects.description: Creates registry directory structure with empty trust ledger and default config.

functions.name: cmd_register
functions.description: Command: `arbiter register <access_graph_path>`. Ingests an access graph JSON file into the registry. Delegates to registry.ingest_access_graph.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

inputs.name: access_graph_path
inputs.description: Path to access_graph.json. Validated by Click as existing file path.

error_cases.name: registry_not_initialized
error_cases.condition: Registry directory does not exist or is missing required files

error_cases.name: schema_violation
error_cases.condition: JSON does not conform to the access graph schema

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: Cannot read the access graph file

preconditions: Registry must be initialized (arbiter init has been run)
access_graph_path must point to a readable JSON file
JSON must conform to access graph schema
postconditions: Access graph nodes and edges are stored in registry
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.description: Reads and parses access graph JSON file.
Persists ingested access graph data to registry.

functions.name: cmd_trust_show
functions.description: Command: `arbiter trust show <node>`. Displays current trust score and history for a node. Delegates to trust_engine.compute_trust_score and trust_engine.get_trust_history.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: The node to query. Validated by NodeIdType ParamType.

error_cases.name: node_not_found
error_cases.condition: node_id does not exist in the access graph

preconditions: Registry must be initialized
node_id must exist in the registered access graph
postconditions: Response contains current raw trust score (never display tier)
History is ordered oldest-first
Trust score computation is deterministic given same ledger input
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.description: Reads trust ledger to compute score and retrieve history.

functions.name: cmd_trust_reset_taint
functions.description: Command: `arbiter trust reset-taint <node> --review <id>`. Clears taint lock on a node, appending a reset event to the trust ledger. Delegates to trust_engine.reset_taint.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: The node to clear taint on. Validated by NodeIdType.

inputs.name: review_id
inputs.description: The review ID authorizing the reset. Required option.

preconditions: Registry must be initialized
node_id must exist in the access graph
Node must currently be tainted
review_id must be non-empty
postconditions: Taint reset event is appended to trust ledger (append-only)
Node is no longer tainted
Trust score is recomputed
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.description: Reads current taint status.
Appends taint reset event to ledger (append-only).
Logs taint reset action with node_id and review_id.

functions.name: cmd_authority_show
functions.description: Command: `arbiter authority show`. Displays the full authority map declared from manifests. Delegates to authority_engine.get_authority_map.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

preconditions: Registry must be initialized
postconditions: Response contains all authority declarations from manifests
Authority is distinct from trust (declared, not computed)
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.description: Reads authority declarations from stored manifests.

functions.name: cmd_blast_radius
functions.description: Command: `arbiter blast-radius <node> <version>`. Computes blast radius via BFS/DFS over access graph edges. Delegates to blast_radius_engine.compute_blast_radius.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: Origin node. Validated by NodeIdType.

inputs.name: version
inputs.description: Version to evaluate. Validated by VersionType.

error_cases.name: node_not_found
error_cases.condition: node_id does not exist in the access graph

error_cases.name: no_access_graph
error_cases.condition: No access graph has been registered

preconditions: Registry must be initialized
Access graph must be registered
node_id must exist in the access graph
postconditions: affected_nodes is ordered by depth ascending
total_affected equals len(affected_nodes)
max_depth equals max depth among affected_nodes or 0 if empty
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.target: registry access graph and trust ledger
structured_side_effects.description: Reads access graph edges for BFS/DFS traversal and trust scores for each node.

performance_budget.complexity: O(V + E) where V=nodes, E=edges in access graph

functions.name: cmd_soak_compute
functions.description: Command: `arbiter soak compute <node> <tier>`. Computes soak duration for a node at a given data classification tier. Delegates to soak_engine.compute_soak_duration.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: Node to compute soak for. Validated by NodeIdType.

inputs.name: tier
inputs.description: Data classification tier. Validated by TierType.

error_cases.name: invalid_tier
error_cases.condition: tier is not a recognized classification tier

preconditions: Registry must be initialized
node_id must exist in the access graph
tier must be a valid classification tier
postconditions: soak_duration_hours >= 0
Computation uses raw trust score (not display tiers)
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.target: registry trust ledger and classification config
structured_side_effects.description: Reads trust score and classification rules.

functions.name: cmd_report
functions.description: Command: `arbiter report --run <run_id>`. Generates a feedback report for a given run. Delegates to report_engine.generate_report.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.description: Run ID to generate report for. Validated by RunIdType.

error_cases.name: run_not_found
error_cases.condition: run_id does not match any known run

preconditions: Registry must be initialized
run_id must correspond to a known run
postconditions: Report file is written to registry reports directory
generated_at is a valid UTC ISO 8601 timestamp
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.description: Reads run data to compile report.
Writes generated report file.

functions.name: cmd_canary_inject
functions.description: Command: `arbiter canary inject --tiers <tier>[,<tier>...]`. Seeds canary data for the specified tiers. Canary fingerprints are structurally valid for tier, globally unique per run, and impossible in real data (UUIDs in domain-shaped strings). Delegates to canary_engine.inject_canaries.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

inputs.name: tiers
inputs.description: Comma-separated list of tiers to inject canary data for. Validated by TierType.

error_cases.name: invalid_tier
error_cases.condition: One or more tiers not recognized

preconditions: Registry must be initialized
tiers must be non-empty
Each tier must be a valid classification tier
postconditions: Canary data is injected for all specified tiers
Each canary is recognizable as synthetic (never plausible real data)
Each canary is globally unique per run
run_id is a new UUID v4
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.description: Persists canary data and run metadata.
Injects synthetic canary records into tier-appropriate locations.

functions.name: cmd_canary_results
functions.description: Command: `arbiter canary results --run <run_id>`. Produces a taint escape report for a canary run. Delegates to canary_engine.get_canary_results.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

inputs.name: run_id
inputs.description: Canary run ID. Validated by RunIdType.

error_cases.name: run_not_found
error_cases.condition: run_id does not correspond to a canary run

preconditions: Registry must be initialized
run_id must correspond to a canary run
postconditions: total_escapes equals len(escapes)
escape_rate equals total_escapes / total_canaries (or 0.0 if total_canaries is 0)
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.description: Reads canary injection records and detected escapes.

functions.name: cmd_watch
functions.description: Command: `arbiter watch [--http-port PORT] [--otlp-port PORT]`. Starts OTLP subscriber and HTTP API in a single process. Blocking command that handles SIGINT/SIGTERM for graceful shutdown. Cold-start target: ready in under 3 seconds. Delegates to otlp_subscriber and http_api components.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

inputs.name: http_port
inputs.description: Port for HTTP API. Defaults to config value.

validators.error_message: HTTP port must be between 1024 and 65535.

inputs.name: otlp_port
inputs.description: Port for OTLP gRPC subscriber. Defaults to config value.

validators.error_message: OTLP port must be between 1024 and 65535.

error_cases.name: port_in_use
error_cases.condition: One or both ports are already bound

error_cases.name: port_conflict
error_cases.condition: http_port and otlp_port are the same

preconditions: Registry must be initialized
http_port and otlp_port must be different
Ports must not be in use
postconditions: OTLP subscriber is listening on otlp_port
HTTP API is listening on http_port
WatchStatus.ready is true before cold_start_timeout_s
On SIGINT/SIGTERM, both servers shut down gracefully
Exit code is SUCCESS_0 on clean shutdown

structured_side_effects.description: Starts HTTP API server accepting JSON requests.
Starts OTLP gRPC subscriber for telemetry ingestion.
Reads registry data for serving API requests and processing telemetry.
Writes trust events and findings based on incoming telemetry.
Logs server lifecycle events and errors.

performance_budget.complexity: O(1) for cold start; O(V+E) per telemetry event processing

functions.name: cmd_findings
functions.description: Command: `arbiter findings --node <node>`. Lists consistency findings for a specific node. Delegates to findings_engine.get_findings.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

inputs.name: node_id
inputs.description: Node to query findings for. Validated by NodeIdType.

error_cases.name: node_not_found
error_cases.condition: node_id does not exist in the access graph

preconditions: Registry must be initialized
node_id must exist in the access graph
postconditions: total equals len(findings)
Each finding includes node_id, severity, and human-readable message
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.description: Reads consistency findings for the specified node.

functions.name: cmd_conflicts
functions.description: Command: `arbiter conflicts [--unresolved]`. Lists conflicts, optionally filtered to only unresolved ones. Delegates to conflicts_engine.list_conflicts.

inputs.name: ctx
inputs.description: Shared CLI context.

inputs.name: unresolved_only
inputs.description: If true, return only unresolved conflicts.

preconditions: Registry must be initialized
postconditions: total equals len(conflicts)
If unresolved_only is true, all returned conflicts have resolved=false
Exit code is SUCCESS_0

structured_side_effects.description: Reads conflict records from registry.

functions.name: format_output
functions.description: Pure helper that formats a Pydantic response model for CLI output. In JSON mode, calls .model_dump(mode='json') and serializes. In text mode, produces a human-readable formatted string. Used by all commands after receiving engine response.

inputs.name: response
inputs.description: Pydantic response model instance (one of the *Response types).

inputs.name: output_format
inputs.description: Resolved output format (text or json, never auto).

preconditions: output_format is 'text' or 'json' (never 'auto')
response is a Pydantic BaseModel instance
postconditions: If json: output is valid JSON matching response.model_dump(mode='json')
If text: output is non-empty human-readable string
No trailing newline added (caller handles)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure formatting function with no side effects.

functions.name: format_error
functions.description: Pure helper that formats a CliError for stderr output. In JSON mode, serializes the CliError model. In text mode, produces 'Error [{error_code}]: {message}' with optional node/field context.

inputs.name: error
inputs.description: The structured error to format.

inputs.name: output_format
inputs.description: Resolved output format (text or json, never auto).

preconditions: output_format is 'text' or 'json' (never 'auto')
postconditions: If json: output is valid JSON matching error.model_dump(mode='json')
If text: output includes error_code and message, plus node_id/field if non-empty
Error messages include the specific node, field, or domain that caused the error

structured_side_effects.description: Pure formatting function with no side effects.

functions.name: map_exception_to_exit_code
functions.description: Pure function used by ArbiterGroup.invoke() to map domain exceptions to ExitCode values. Maps known exception types to their corresponding exit codes per the ExitCode enum.

inputs.name: exception
inputs.description: The caught exception instance.

postconditions: Returns DOMAIN_ERROR_1 for domain logic exceptions
Returns USAGE_ERROR_2 for validation/input exceptions
Returns IO_ERROR_3 for filesystem/network exceptions
Returns NOT_FOUND_4 for entity-not-found exceptions
Returns DOMAIN_ERROR_1 for unrecognized exceptions (safe default)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure mapping function with no side effects.

functions.name: resolve_output_format
functions.description: Pure function that resolves OutputFormat.auto to text or json based on TTY detection. Called during CliContext construction.

inputs.name: requested_format
inputs.description: The user-requested format (may be auto).

inputs.name: is_tty
inputs.description: Whether stdout is a TTY.

postconditions: If requested_format is 'text' or 'json', returns it unchanged
If requested_format is 'auto' and is_tty is true, returns 'text'
If requested_format is 'auto' and is_tty is false, returns 'json'
Return value is never 'auto'

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

invariants: CLI is a thin adapter layer — all business logic is delegated to engine dependencies
Trust and authority are never conflated: trust commands use trust_engine, authority commands use authority_engine
Trust is earned (computed from ledger), authority is declared (from manifests)
All policy calculations use raw trust score, never display tiers
The trust ledger is append-only — no updates, no deletes
Canary patterns are always recognizable as synthetic (never plausible real data)
All errors written to stderr, all normal output to stdout
JSON output is always .model_dump(mode='json') of the Pydantic response model
Error messages always include the specific node, field, or domain that caused the error
Exit codes are deterministic for a given error condition
CliContext.output_format is never 'auto' after resolution in group callback
No async — all operations are synchronous
Every command is testable via CliRunner with mocked engine dependencies
Each CLI module file is under 300 lines
Custom ParamTypes (NodeIdType, RunIdType, TierType, VersionType) delegate validation to corresponding Pydantic types/enums
ArbiterGroup.invoke() wraps super().invoke() in try/except for centralized error handling
Watch command cold start completes in under 3 seconds
